Hevea brasiliensis

Jarum-jarum

Ixora congesta

Senduduk

Melastoma malabathricum

Beccari's seagrass

Halophila beccarii

Seagrass with the smallest leaves, 0.5cm wide, emerging in a rosette of 5-10 leaves. Common on Chek Jawa.

Hairy spoon seagrass

Halophila decipiens

Spoon shaped leaves, longer and the width, 0.5 wide, 1-2.5cm long, emerging in pairs. Minute serrations on edges and minute hairs on both sides of the leaves. Seen in deeper waters in our Southern islands.

Tape seagrass

Enhalus acoroides

Longest seagrass with ribbon-like leaves. 30cm-1.5m long, 1-2cm wide. Thick rhizomes. Flowering often observed. Large female flowers on coiled stalk. Tiny white male flowers produced from inflorescence at base of the plant. Often seen on most of our undisturbed shores. Largest meadow on Pulau Semakau.

Needle seagrass

Halodule

Thin ribbon-like leaves up to 15cm long and 3.5mm wide. Rhizomes thin. Sometimes seen on some our shores, especially on sand bars. Luxuriant growths seen on seaward sandbars on Chek Jawa.

Spoon seagrass

Halophila ovalis

Spoon shaped leaves, 0.5-1cm wide, 1-4cm long, emerging in pairs. Common on many of our shores.

Smooth ribbon seagrass

Cymodocea rotundata

Narrow and shorter ribbon-like leaves with rounded or squarish tips without serrations. Rhizomes thick and smooth. Seen on Chek Jawa, Tanah Merah and Cyrene Reef.

Seagrasses

Serrated ribbon seagrass

Cymodocea serrulata

Narrow and shorter ribbon-like leaves with rounded tips with fine serrations. Rhizomes thick and smooth. Seen on some of our Southern reefs and shores.

Fern seagrass

Halophila spinulosa

2cm wide, 4cm long. Only seagrass with opposite leaves on a stem that makes it appear fern-like. 10-20 opposite pairs of leaves on one stem, the stem about 4cm long. Rhizomes thin, sometimes woody. Common on Chek Jawa and Changi.

Noodle seagrass

Syringodium isoetifolium

Leaves cylindrical (like plastic tubing). Common on on Pulau Semakau.

Sickle seagrass

Thalassia hemprichii

Shorter and broader ribbon-like leaves that are curved into strap sickle shapes (4-11mm wide and 10-40cm long). Rhizomes thick. Small patch on Chek Jawa, larger meadows on Labrador, scattered clumps on some of our other Southern shores.
